final_answer:
Right now, no per-validator manual whitelist is required in code; join is permissionless at entry, and final acceptance is by validator vote (>2/3 voting power), not an explicit “us approve this validator” hook.

Why this matters: today’s behavior is “open staking + consensus verification,” so if you run all validators you effectively control admission anyway, but there is no dedicated admin approval gate in the join path.

How it works now (from code):
- `stakeFor/stakeForPOL` are public (`onlyWhenUnlocked`, not `onlyGovernance`) in [StakeManager.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/pos-contracts/contracts/staking/stakeManager/StakeManager.sol#L374), with gates: slot cap `validatorThreshold`, `amount >= minDeposit`, and `fee >= minGiltConsensusFee` in [StakeManager.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/pos-contracts/contracts/staking/stakeManager/StakeManager.sol#L402).
- Defaults are `minDeposit = 1e18`, `minGiltConsensusFee = 1e18`, `validatorThreshold = 7` in [StakeManager.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/pos-contracts/contracts/staking/stakeManager/StakeManager.sol#L99).
- Bridge processor turns the L1 stake event into `MsgValidatorJoin` in [stake.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/processor/stake.go#L158).
- GiltConsensus only applies join if side-tx is approved; otherwise it is skipped/rejected in [side_msg_server.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/side_msg_server.go#L600).
- Approval is by vote-extension tally: `YES > 2/3` voting power in [vote_ext_utils.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/app/vote_ext_utils.go#L392) and then post-handler runs with `VOTE_YES` in [abci.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/app/abci.go#L703).

Next step: if you want strict “needs our acceptance,” add an explicit governance/allowlist gate in `StakeManager._stakeFor` (and optionally mirror that check in stake side-handler) instead of relying only on slot/minimum/vote rules.
